subject: Databases
title: Availability and Redundancy
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Availability and Redundancy.

hook.text: Your only database server dies at 2 AM. The entire application is down. No failover, no backup ready to go.

buildup.text: A single server is a single point of failure. Hardware fails, data centers lose power, networks break.

discovery.text: Replicas keep copies of data on separate machines. If one dies, another takes over immediately.

twist.text: Availability isn't just about crashes. Planned maintenance needs rolling updates. Zero downtime requires replicas.

climax.text: 99.99% uptime allows only 52 minutes of downtime per year. Achieving that demands distributed architecture.

punchline.text: Redundancy prevents outages. Multiple copies mean no single point of failure.

quiz.question: How do replicas improve database availability?
options: If one server fails, another replica can immediately take over and serve requests
Replicas make queries run faster by compressing data
Each replica stores a different portion of the data
